generatedSentence: Elias, a grownup worker with an iconic past in industrial design, had long embraced an isolationist retreat among the mountains. His remote cabin, a constant renovation project with exposed walls and a creaking gate, served as his sanctuary. He spent his days using salvaged machinery and strange utensils to meticulously construct an intricate nanotechnology instrument. His only link to the outside was an antique radio and occasional calls to his elderly grandparent. He enjoyed the quiet solitude, often reflecting in a flashback haze, a serene fantasy life. Inside, his sparse pantry held essentials, a single packet of noodle, and a spice shaker. Despite the simple hygiene and close proximity to his work, a sense of lonely wishful thinking pervaded his evenings, often spent in a comfortable nightgown. A small mousetrap in the corner indicated its rural location. One night, a colossal typhoon struck, a true natural fatality in the making, causing a massive power wipeout and plunging the region into darkness. Elias, normally unflappable, felt a jolt of rage. The storm’s voltage surges whacked his prototype, sending sparks and neutrons flying. His carefully designed orbital systems, meant for outstanding outdoor power generation, were failing. He knew it was an illegal risk to be running such experimental tech, but his desperate wants for clean energy had overridden his caution. The kerfuffle outside intensified, sounding like a hundred angry dwarves hammering. He donned heavy gloves, ready to adjust the damaged systems, a desperate odyssey beginning. A sudden jingle from a loose piece of equipment, a sound he had rocked to many times during testing, now felt like a death knell. He needed to hydrate and keep a poker face through the escalating crisis, realizing his karma was perhaps catching up to him. He blinked, assessing the damage, as an intense sense of loneliness set in. Driven by an energetic resolve, Elias decided to make his stand, almost like an anarchist against the forces of nature. He had to abandon the failing systems and quickly implement his arcane backup plan: a series of ultrasonic testers to recalibrate the grid. He worked with fierce valour, dodging falling debris as the storm howled. His hands moved quickly, knotting wires, a desperate race against time. He recalled a hazzan-like figure, a mentor from his past, who taught him to never give up, and the whole situation felt like a dangerous illusion. He remembered a youth spent kidding around on an offroad ride along the old motorway. Now, the stakes were life and death. He needed to activate the twenty emergency jumpers to bypass the occlusion in the power grid. He pulled out a small cam that held the crucial override codes, feeling the intense pressure. He took a cola break, a moment of respite, then quickly re-engaged, focusing on the marked circuits. He knew his efforts, if successful, would earn him no medal or salary, but the sheer majestic accomplishment would be enough. The air was thick with spore-like dust from the damage. He cocked his head, listening to the groaning structure. As the storm finally began to rewind, Elias, drenched but happily exhausted, finally allowed himself to layback on a makeshift pillow near the humming system. He had won. The power was restored, though his cabin was still a mess of scattered wares. He thought of an oriental tea set he'd once admired, a symbol of peace. He smiled, despite the lingering sense of bigotry he sometimes felt from the outside world concerning his unusual projects. He knew that for all his inventiveness, some things, like the need for a good grocery run, remained unchanged. He pictured himself pushing a cart, perhaps finding a special pearl in the discount bin or a fresh bunch of kale. He still had some artic bears branded freezers full of ice to sort through. He wasn't waiting for accolades, but perhaps a small raffle prize would be nice. He stretched, feeling his weary bones. He thought about adjusting his bicycle's kickstand, after the clean-up. He deserved a moment of peace, perhaps even a few imaginary kisses of gratitude for his efforts. The memory of the locust-like storm fading, he felt a sense of moderate accomplishment. He then gently whisked away the dust from a small model of old wagons, a symbol of perseverance, and finally allowed himself to rest.
panelSentences: Elias, a grownup worker with an iconic past in industrial design, had long embraced an isolationist retreat among the mountains. His remote cabin, a constant renovation project with exposed walls and a creaking gate, served as his sanctuary. He spent his days using salvaged machinery and strange utensils to meticulously construct an intricate nanotechnology instrument. His only link to the outside was an antique radio and occasional calls to his elderly grandparent. He enjoyed the quiet solitude, often reflecting in a flashback haze, a serene fantasy life. Inside, his sparse pantry held essentials, a single packet of noodle, and a spice shaker. Despite the simple hygiene and close proximity to his work, a sense of lonely wishful thinking pervaded his evenings, often spent in a comfortable nightgown. A small mousetrap in the corner indicated its rural location.

One night, a colossal typhoon struck, a true natural fatality in the making, causing a massive power wipeout and plunging the region into darkness. Elias, normally unflappable, felt a jolt of rage. The storm’s voltage surges whacked his prototype, sending sparks and neutrons flying. His carefully designed orbital systems, meant for outstanding outdoor power generation, were failing. He knew it was an illegal risk to be running such experimental tech, but his desperate wants for clean energy had overridden his caution. The kerfuffle outside intensified, sounding like a hundred angry dwarves hammering. He donned heavy gloves, ready to adjust the damaged systems, a desperate odyssey beginning. A sudden jingle from a loose piece of equipment, a sound he had rocked to many times during testing, now felt like a death knell. He needed to hydrate and keep a poker face through the escalating crisis, realizing his karma was perhaps catching up to him. He blinked, assessing the damage, as an intense sense of loneliness set in.

Driven by an energetic resolve, Elias decided to make his stand, almost like an anarchist against the forces of nature. He had to abandon the failing systems and quickly implement his arcane backup plan: a series of ultrasonic testers to recalibrate the grid. He worked with fierce valour, dodging falling debris as the storm howled. His hands moved quickly, knotting wires, a desperate race against time. He recalled a hazzan-like figure, a mentor from his past, who taught him to never give up, and the whole situation felt like a dangerous illusion. He remembered a youth spent kidding around on an offroad ride along the old motorway. Now, the stakes were life and death. He needed to activate the twenty emergency jumpers to bypass the occlusion in the power grid. He pulled out a small cam that held the crucial override codes, feeling the intense pressure. He took a cola break, a moment of respite, then quickly re-engaged, focusing on the marked circuits. He knew his efforts, if successful, would earn him no medal or salary, but the sheer majestic accomplishment would be enough. The air was thick with spore-like dust from the damage. He cocked his head, listening to the groaning structure.

As the storm finally began to rewind, Elias, drenched but happily exhausted, finally allowed himself to layback on a makeshift pillow near the humming system. He had won. The power was restored, though his cabin was still a mess of scattered wares. He thought of an oriental tea set he'd once admired, a symbol of peace. He smiled, despite the lingering sense of bigotry he sometimes felt from the outside world concerning his unusual projects. He knew that for all his inventiveness, some things, like the need for a good grocery run, remained unchanged. He pictured himself pushing a cart, perhaps finding a special pearl in the discount bin or a fresh bunch of kale. He still had some artic bears branded freezers full of ice to sort through. He wasn't waiting for accolades, but perhaps a small raffle prize would be nice. He stretched, feeling his weary bones. He thought about adjusting his bicycle's kickstand, after the clean-up. He deserved a moment of peace, perhaps even a few imaginary kisses of gratitude for his efforts. The memory of the locust-like storm fading, he felt a sense of moderate accomplishment. He then gently whisked away the dust from a small model of old wagons, a symbol of perseverance, and finally allowed himself to rest.

He took a **cola** break, a moment of respite, then quickly re-engaged, focusing on the **marked** circuits. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, neo-noir aesthetic with high contrast, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Character: an older, rugged man with a dense salt-and-pepper beard, wearing a worn mechanic's jumpsuit and heavy work boots. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The man intently tinkering with complex, glowing circuits on a workbench, his brow furrowed in concentration. Close-up shot on his hands and focused face. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The man standing by a shattered window, gazing out at a raging typhoon, rain lashing against the glass, a grim determination on his face. Medium shot from inside, showcasing the storm's fury. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The man, wearing heavy gloves, frantically connecting thick, sparking wires within a damaged power grid, dynamic low-angle shot, action-oriented. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The man, exhausted but victorious, leaning back on a makeshift pillow beside the now stably humming, repaired machinery, a faint smile on his weary, wrinkled face. Wide shot showcasing him and the restored power system. Add cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and dramatic cinematic lighting throughout.

definitions.definition: A shelly concretion, usually rounded, and having a brilliant luster, with varying tints, found in the mantle, or between the mantle and shell, of certain bivalve mollusks, especially in the pearl oysters and river mussels, and sometimes in certain univalves. It is usually due to a secretion of shelly substance around some irritating foreign particle. Its substance is the same as nacre, or mother-of-pearl. Round lustrous pearls are used in jewellery.
Something precious.
A capsule of gelatin or similar substance containing liquid for e.g. medicinal application.
Nacre, or mother-of-pearl.
A whitish speck or film on the eye.
A fish allied to the turbot; the brill.
A light-colored tern.
One of the circle of tubercles which form the bur on a deer's antler.
The size of type between diamond and agate, standardized as 5-point.
A fringe or border.
A jewel or gem.
The clitoris.
(sometimes figurative) To set or adorn with pearls, or with mother-of-pearl.
To cause to resemble pearls in shape; to make into small round grains.
definitions.example: to pearl barley

definitions.definition: To cause to resemble pearls in lustre or iridescence.
To resemble pearl or pearls.
To hunt for pearls
definitions.example: to go pearling

definitions.definition: To dig the nose of one's surfboard into the water, often on takeoff.
The act of colliding or crashing.
definitions.example: The skateboarder had a nasty wipeout as she came off the ramp.

definitions.definition: Total destruction or elimination.
definitions.example: The wipeout of this industry could be catastrophic.

definitions.definition: A person who performs labor for a living, especially manual labor.
A nonreproductive social insect, such as ant, bee, termite, or wasp.
A thread performing one instance of a particular task within a program.
definitions.example: This FTP client spawns a separate worker for each file to be uploaded.

definitions.definition: To hit, slap or strike.
To kill, bump off.
To share or parcel out; often with up.
definitions.example: to whack the spoils of a robbery

definitions.definition: To beat convincingly; to thrash.
(usually in the negative) To surpass; to better.
Tired; fatigued.
definitions.example: After that all-night party, we were all whacked.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated on drugs.
A rampart of earth, stones etc. built up for defensive purposes.
A structure built for defense surrounding a city, castle etc.
definitions.example: The town wall was surrounded by a moat.

definitions.definition: Each of the substantial structures acting either as the exterior of or divisions within a structure.
definitions.example: We're adding another wall in this room during the remodeling.  The wind blew against the walls of the tent.

definitions.definition: A point of desperation.
A point of defeat or extinction.
An impediment to free movement.
definitions.example: A wall of police officers met the protesters before they reached the capitol steps.

definitions.definition: A type of butterfly (Lasiommata megera).
(often in combination) A barrier.
definitions.example: a seawall;  a firewall

definitions.definition: A barrier to vision.
Something with the apparent solidity and dimensions of a building wall.
definitions.example: a wall of sound;  a wall of water

definitions.definition: A divisive or containing structure in an organ or cavity.
(auction) A fictional bidder used to increase the price at an auction.
A doctor who tries to admit as few patients as possible.
A line of defenders set up between an opposing free-kick taker and the goal.
A personal notice board listing messages of interest to a particular user.
To enclose with, or as if with, a wall or walls.
definitions.example: He walled the study with books.

definitions.definition: To boil.
To well, as water; spring.
A spring of water.
A kind of knot often used at the end of a rope; a wall knot or wale.
To make a wall knot on the end of (a rope).
To utilize or employ.
To accustom; to habituate. (Now common only in participial form. Uses the same pronunciation as the noun; see usage notes.)
definitions.example: soldiers who are used to hardships and danger

definitions.definition: (except in past tense) To habitually do; to be wont to do. (Now chiefly in past-tense forms; see used to.)
definitions.example: I used to get things done.

definitions.definition: To behave toward; to act with regard to; to treat.
definitions.example: to use an animal cruelly

definitions.definition: To behave, act, comport oneself.
Use; utilization
An instrument or device for domestic use, especially in the kitchen.
definitions.example: We have convenient storage for all the kitchen utensils.

definitions.definition: A useful small tool, implement, or vessel.
definitions.example: He stocked up on old-style writing utensils.

definitions.definition: The science and technology of creating nanoparticles and of manufacturing machines which have sizes within the range of nanometres.
A long loose robe worn mainly by women for sleeping in.
A dressing gown.
One who believes in or advocates the absence of hierarchy and authority in most forms (compare anarchism), especially one who works toward the realization of such.
One who disregards laws and social norms as a form of rebellion against authority.
(by extension) One who promotes chaos and lawlessness; a nihilist.
One who resents outside control or influence on his or her life, in particular a government, and therefore desires the absence of political control.
(somewhat rare) Relating to anarchism or to anarchists, anarchistic.
To give up or relinquish control of, to surrender or to give oneself over, or to yield to one's emotions.
To desist in doing, practicing, following, holding, or adhering to; to turn away from; to permit to lapse; to renounce; to discontinue.
To leave behind; to desert as in a ship or a position, typically in response to overwhelming odds or impending dangers; to forsake, in spite of a duty or responsibility.
definitions.example: Many baby girls have been abandoned on the streets of Beijing.

definitions.definition: To subdue; to take control of.
To cast out; to banish; to expel; to reject.
To no longer exercise a right, title, or interest, especially with no interest of reclaiming it again; to yield; to relinquish.
To surrender to the insurer (an insured item), so as to claim a total loss.
To modify.
definitions.example: Morimoto's recipes are adjusted to suit the American palate.

definitions.definition: To improve or rectify.
definitions.example: He adjusted his initial conclusion to reflect the new data.

definitions.definition: To settle an insurance claim.
To change to fit circumstances.
definitions.example: Most immigrants adjust quickly to a new community.   She waited for her eyes to adjust to the darkness.

definitions.definition: Understood by only a few.
definitions.example: arcane rituals

definitions.definition: (by extension) Obscure, mysterious.
definitions.example: arcane details

definitions.definition: Requiring secret or mysterious knowledge to understand.
Extremely old (e.g. interpretation or knowledge), and possibly irrelevant.
definitions.example: An arcane law

definitions.definition: To be, or act as, a grandfather to.
To retain discontinued laws or rules for (a thing, person or organization previously affected by them).
The parent of someone's parent
A doorlike structure outside a house.
Doorway, opening, or passage in a fence or wall.
Movable barrier.
definitions.example: The gate in front of the railroad crossing went up after the train had passed.

definitions.definition: A logical pathway made up of switches which turn on or off. Examples are and, or, nand, etc.
The gap between a batsman's bat and pad.
definitions.example: Singh was bowled through the gate, a very disappointing way for a world-class batsman to get out.

definitions.definition: The amount of money made by selling tickets to a concert or a sports event.
(flow cytometry) A line that separates particle type-clusters on two-dimensional dot plots.
Passageway (as in an air terminal) where passengers can embark or disembark.
The controlling terminal of a field effect transistor (FET).
In a lock tumbler, the opening for the stump of the bolt to pass through or into.
The channel or opening through which metal is poured into the mould; the ingate.
The waste piece of metal cast in the opening; a sprue or sullage piece. Also written geat and git.
A mechanism, in a film camera and projector, that holds each frame momentarily stationary behind the aperture.
A tally mark consisting of four vertical bars crossed by a diagonal, representing a count of five.
To keep something inside by means of a closed gate.
To punish, especially a child or teenager, by not allowing them to go out.
To open a closed ion channel.
To furnish with a gate.
To turn (an image intensifier) on and off selectively as needed, or to avoid damage. See autogating.
Anything that seems to be something that it is not.
definitions.example: Using artificial additives, scientists can create the illusion of fruit flavours in food.

definitions.definition: A misapprehension; a belief in something that is in fact not true.
definitions.example: Jane has this illusion that John is in love with her.

definitions.definition: A magician’s trick.
The state of being deceived or misled.
A device used to produce music.
definitions.example: The violinist was a master of her instrument.

definitions.definition: A means or agency for achieving an effect.
A measuring or displaying device.
definitions.example: The instrument detected an increase in radioactivity.

definitions.definition: A tool, implement used for manipulation or measurement.
definitions.example: The dentist set down his tray of instruments.

definitions.definition: A legal document, such as a contract, deed, trust, mortgage, power, indenture, or will.
definitions.example: A bond indenture is the instrument that gives a bond its value.

definitions.definition: A person used as a mere tool for achieving a goal.
To apply measuring devices.
To devise, conceive, cook up, plan.
To perform upon an instrument; to prepare for an instrument.
definitions.example: a sonata instrumented for orchestra

definitions.definition: The technology that allows for the transmission of sound or other signals by modulation of electromagnetic waves.
A device that can capture (receive) the signal sent over radio waves and render the modulated signal as sound.
On-board entertainment system in a car, usually including a radio receiver as well as the capability to play audio from recorded media.
A device that can transmit radio signals.
The continuous broadcasting of sound via the Internet in the style of traditional radio.
To use two-way radio to transmit (a message) (to another radio or other radio operator).
definitions.example: I think the boat is sinking; we'd better radio for help. / I radioed him already. / Radio the coordinates this time. / OK. I radioed them the coordinates.

definitions.definition: To order or assist (to a location), using telecommunications.
The act of rewinding.
A button or other mechanism for rewinding.
definitions.example: I meant to pause the picture, but hit the rewind by mistake.

definitions.definition: To wind (something) again.
To wind (something) back, now especially of cassette or video tape, CD, DVD etc.; to go back on a video or audio recording.
To go back or think back to a previous moment or place, or a previous point in a discourse.
A device for capturing or killing mice and other rodents.
A website designed to open another copy of itself when the user tries to close the webpage. Frequently used by advertisers and pornographers.
Ordinary, everyday cheese.
A slice of bread or toast topped with cheese and then grilled or microwaved.
An antisubmarine rocket used mainly during World War II by the US Navy and US Coast Guard.
To trap; to trick or fool (someone) into a bad situation.
To prevent (the user) from leaving a website by opening another copy when it is closed.

(parts of Australia) A broad highway designed for high speed traffic, having restrictions on the vehicle types permitted and merging lanes instead of cross traffic; in parts of the United States and other places called freeway.
The difference in electrostatic potential between two points in space, especially between live and neutral conductors or the earth.
definitions.example: Be careful when opening high-voltage equipment.

definitions.definition: To form into a knot; to tie with a knot or knots.
definitions.example: We knotted the ends of the rope to keep it from unravelling.

definitions.definition: To form wrinkles in the forehead, as a sign of concentration, concern, surprise, etc.
definitions.example: She knotted her brow in concentration while attempting to unravel the tangled strands.

definitions.definition: To unite closely; to knit together.
To entangle or perplex; to puzzle.
To form knots.
To knit knots for a fringe.
The formation of a knot.
The swelling of the bulbus glandis.
Possessing or pertaining to energy.
definitions.example: Cosmic rays are energetic particles from outer space.

definitions.definition: Characterised by force or vigour; full of energy; lively, vigorous.
definitions.example: Those kids are so energetic: they want to run around playing all day.

definitions.definition: Having powerful effects; efficacious, potent.
A precious stone, especially an orient pearl.
A member or descendant of the peoples and cultures of the Orient.
A lily cultivar of a widely varied group, with strong scent.
A person from the eastern region of a place.
Of a pearl or other precious stone: having a superior lustre.
Pertaining to the eastern part of the sky; happening before sunrise.
Happening in the eastern part of a given place or location.
Pertaining to the regions east of the Mediterranean, beyond the Roman Empire or the early Christian world; of the Near East, the Middle East or the Far East, now especially relating to East Asia.
Designating various types of aromatic tobacco grown in Turkey and the Balkans.
The state proceeding from destiny; invincible necessity, superior to, and independent of, free and rational control.
Tendency to death, destruction or danger, as if by decree of fate.
That which is decreed by fate or which is fatal; a fatal event.
Death.
An accident that causes death.
A person killed.
A move where one character kills another.
A spin in which the head and shoulders are dropped backwards and the back arched downwards toward the ice. Also called a layback spin
A maneuver to scale a vertical crack, by pulling with the arms and jamming the legs underneath, and shuffling up on limb at a time.
A surf maneuver where the upright surfer leans back against the wave.
A trick where the boarder leans back while on the lip of the ramp, or down a rail.
(audio editing) The process of recording the final sound onto the master track.
A short backwards pass to a teammate in an attacking position
A wedge-shaped kerbstone that allows a vehicle to enter a driveway without a bump
To climb using layback maneuvers.
The science of health, its promotion and preservation.
Those conditions and practices that promote and preserve health.
definitions.example: Hygiene is an important consideration in places where food is prepared.

definitions.definition: Cleanliness.
definitions.example: They have poor personal hygiene.

definitions.definition: (of a macro) The property of having an expansion that is guaranteed not to cause the accidental capture of identifiers.
Closeness; the state of being near as in space, time, or relationship.
definitions.example: The proximity of the heat source allowed it to be detected by the sensor.

definitions.definition: A specification of an object or process, referring to requirements to be satisfied and thus conditions to be met for them to solve a problem.
A plan (with more or less detail) for the structure and functions of an artifact, building or system.
A pattern, as an element of a work of art or architecture.
The composition of a work of art.
Intention or plot.
definitions.example: To be hateful of the truth by design.

definitions.definition: The shape or appearance given to an object, especially one that is intended to make it more attractive.
The art of designing
definitions.example: Danish furniture design is world-famous.

definitions.definition: To plan and carry out (a picture, work of art, construction etc.).
To plan (to do something).
definitions.example: The king designed to mount an expedition to the New World.

definitions.definition: To assign, appoint (something to someone); to designate.
To mark out and exhibit; to designate; to indicate; to show; to point out; to appoint.
To manifest requirements to be satisfied by an object or process for them to solve a problem.
definitions.example: The client had me create new designs until they were satisfied with one.

definitions.definition: A desire, wish, longing.
(often followed by of) Lack, absence.
Poverty.
Something needed or desired; a thing of which the loss is felt.
A depression in coal strata, hollowed out before the subsequent deposition took place.
To wish for or desire (something); to feel a need or desire for; to crave or demand.
definitions.example: What do you want to eat?  I want you to leave.  I never wanted to go back to live with my mother.

definitions.definition: (in particular) To wish, desire or demand to see, have the presence of or do business with.
definitions.example: Danish police want him for embezzlement.

definitions.definition: To desire (to experience desire); to wish.
definitions.example: You can leave if you want.

definitions.definition: (usually second person, often future tense) To be advised to do something (compare should, ought).
definitions.example: You’ll want to repeat this three or four times to get the best result.

definitions.definition: (now colloquial) To lack and be in need of or require (something, such as a noun or verbal noun).
definitions.example: That chair wants fixing.

definitions.definition: (now rare) To have occasion for (something requisite or useful); to require or need.
To be lacking or deficient or absent.
